title: To Fortunatus, Bishop of Neapolis (Naples).

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Inasmuch as the Father of God’s servants whom I had sent to the city of Naples has, by the ordering of God as it hath pleased Him, departed this life, it has seemed good to me to send the bearer of these presents, the monk Barbatianus, for the government of the same monks.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For the present we decide that he shall be Prior, so that, if his life should approve itself to thy Fraternity, thou mayest after a little time ordain him as their Father.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For he has some good qualities that commend him.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But he has this great fault, that he is exceedingly wise in his own conceit.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And it is evidently known how many branches of sin may spring from this root.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let thy Holiness, therefore, keep careful watch over him; and if you shall find him become wary in government and humble in his own mind, then, with the permission of God, advance him to the dignity of Abbot.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if he makes little progress in humility, defer his ordination, and report to me.
